[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兄弟中更相亲 - Indicates a closer relationship than usual among brothers.
  • 外家皆关中贵族 - All the maternal families are nobles of Guanzhong.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 肅宗在春宮 - Indicates that Emperor Suzong was in the position of Crown Prince (春宮), which is a title given to the heir apparent.
  • 従玄宗诣太清宫 - Means 'followed Emperor Xuanzong to the Taqing Palace,' where '诣' means to go or visit.
  • 有龙见于殿之东梁 - Describes a dragon appearing on the eastern beam of the palace hall, which is an unusual and symbolic event.
  • 真我儿也 - Translates to 'truly my son,' indicating approval or recognition.
  • 安禄山入觐 - Means 'An Lushan came to court,' where '觐' refers to the act of a vassal or subject paying homage to the emperor.
  • 吾非不疑，但此胡无尾，汝姑置之 - A statement by Emperor Xuanzong, meaning 'I am not without suspicion, but this barbarian has no tail, so you might as well leave him alone,' which is a metaphorical way of saying he is not a threat.
  • 政和公主，肃宗第三女也 - Introduces Princess Zhenghe as the third daughter of Emperor Suzong.
  • 降柳潭 - Means 'married into the Liu family,' where '降' is used here to mean marriage.
  • 女优有弄假官戏 - Refers to a performance involving actors playing the roles of officials, which was a form of entertainment.
  • 参军妆 - Describes a specific makeup style for the character known as 'Shangjun,' often a comedic role.
  • 阿布思伏法 - Indicates that Abosi was executed according to the law.
  • 配掖庭 - Means 'assigned to the imperial harem,' where '掖庭' is the area within the palace where concubines and servants lived.
  • 善为优 - Means 'skilled in performing,' where '优' refers to performers or entertainers.
  • 隶乐工 - Means 'assigned to the musicians,' where '乐工' refers to musicians or performers.
  • 遂为假官之长 - Indicates that she became the leader of the actors playing officials.
  • 上及侍宴者笑乐 - Means 'the emperor and those attending the banquet laughed and enjoyed themselves.'
  • 俯首颦眉不视 - Describes the princess lowering her head and frowning, not looking up, which shows her disapproval.
  • 若果冤横 - Means 'if indeed wronged,' referring to the possibility that Abosi was unjustly executed.
  • 岂忍使其妻与群优杂处为笑谑之具哉 - A rhetorical question asking if it is right to make the wife of a wronged person a subject of ridicule.
  • 深以为不可 - Means 'deeply feels it is not acceptable,' expressing the princess's strong disapproval.
  • 上亦悯恻 - Means 'the emperor also felt pity and sorrow.'
  • 遂罢戏，而免阿布思之妻 - Indicates that the performance was stopped, and Abosi's wife was released from her assignment.
  • 由是贤重公主 - Means 'from this, the princess was highly regarded,' indicating that her actions earned her respect.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong of Tang, the son of Emperor Xuanzong and the fourth emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, the father of Emperor Suzong and one of the most famous emperors of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太清宫 - A temple dedicated to the worship of the Taoist deity Laozi, located in Luoyang.
  • 安禄山 - A powerful military leader who later rebelled against the Tang Dynasty, leading to the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 阿布思 - A Turkic general who was executed during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 政和公主 - A princess of the Tang Dynasty, the third daughter of Emperor Suzong.
  • 柳潭 - A noble family to which Princess Zhenghe was married.
  • 女优 - Female performers, often dancers or actresses in traditional Chinese theater.
  • 弄假官戏 - A type of theatrical performance where actors played the roles of officials, often satirical or comedic.
  • 参军妆 - A specific makeup style for the 'Shangjun' role, a comedic character in traditional Chinese theater.
  • 掖庭 - The area within the palace where concubines and servants lived.
  • 乐工 - Musicians or performers in the imperial court.

Proper Nouns

  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong of Tang.
  • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 太清宫 - Taqing Palace.
  • 安禄山 - An Lushan.
  • 萧宗 - Crown Prince Li Heng (later Emperor Suzong).
  • 政和公主 - Princess Zhenghe.
  • 柳潭 - Liu family.
  • 阿布思 - Abosi.
  • 掖庭 - Imperial harem.
  • 乐工 - Musicians or performers in the imperial court.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 代宗獨孤妃薨 - Formal way to report the death of an imperial consort.
  • 贈貞懿皇后 - Posthumous title given to the deceased empress.
  • 尚父汾陽王 - Title combining a noble rank and a familial honorific, indicating a high-ranking official and a respected elder.
  • 以其子尚主之故 - Explanation of the reason for the Duke's involvement.
  • 將葬 - Indicates the upcoming funeral.
  • 欲致祭 - Expresses the Duke's intention to perform a ritual.
  • 自古無人臣祭皇后之儀 - Highlights the novelty and potential controversy of the proposed action.
  • 此事須得柳侍御裁之 - Indicates the need for expert advice.
  • 時予外伯祖殿中侍御史 - Introduces the key figure who provides the solution.
  • 掌汾陽書記 - Describes his role.
  • 奉使在京 - Explains his presence in the capital.
  • 即以書急召之 - Describes the urgency of the situation.
  • 既至 - Marks the transition to the next part of the story.
  • 汾陽迎笑曰 - Describes the Duke's reaction.
  • 有切事 - Emphasizes the importance of the issue.
  • 須藉侍御为之 - Reiterates the need for his expertise.
  • 遂说祭事 - Describes the discussion.
  • 殿中君初亦对如诸人 - Shows his initial hesitation.
  • 既而曰 - Marks a change in his opinion.
  • 礼缘人情 - Explains his reasoning.
  • 令公勋德不同常人 - Highlights the Duke's unique status.
  • 且又为国姻戚 - Adds another layer of significance.
  • 自令公始 - Indicates the beginning of a new practice.
  • 亦谓得宜 - Concludes his argument.
  • 汾阳曰 - Marks the Duke's agreement.
  • 正合子仪本意 - Confirms the Duke's approval.
  • 殿中君草祭文 - Describes his task.
  • 其官衔之首称 - Introduces the specific wording.
  • 驸马都尉郭暧父 - Specific title used.
  • 其中叙特恩许致祭之意 - Describes the content of the text.
  • 辞简礼备 - Praises the quality of the text.
  • 汾阳览之大喜 - Describes his reaction.
  • 其文列于左 - Indicates that the text will follow.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 代宗 - Emperor Daizong of Tang, who reigned from 762 to 779.
  • 独孤妃 - Empress Du, a consort of Emperor Daizong.
  • 汾阳王 - The Duke of Fenyang, a high-ranking official and military leader, likely referring to Guo Ziyi.
  • 郭暧 - Prince Consort De, the son of Guo Ziyi, who married a princess.
  • 柳侍御 - Counselor Liu, likely referring to Liu Yan, a prominent official and historian.
  • 殿中侍御史 - A high-ranking official in the Tang court, responsible for various administrative duties.
  • 驸马都尉 - A title given to the husbands of imperial princesses.
  • 特恩 - Special imperial favor or decree.
  • 致祭 - A ritual of offering sacrifices or tributes, often performed to honor the deceased.
  • 官衔 - Official titles or ranks.
  • 祭文 - A text used in rituals to honor the deceased.

Proper Nouns

  • 代宗 - Emperor Daizong of Tang.
  • 独孤妃 - Empress Du.
  • 贞懿皇后 - Posthumous title of Empress Du.
  • 汾阳王 - The Duke of Fenyang (Guo Ziyi).
  • 郭暧 - Prince Consort De.
  • 柳侍御 - Counselor Liu (likely Liu Yan).
  • 殿中侍御史 - A high-ranking official in the Tang court.
  • 驸马都尉 - A title given to the husbands of imperial princesses.
  • 特恩 - Special imperial favor or decree.
  • 致祭 - A ritual of offering sacrifices or tributes.
  • 官衔 - Official titles or ranks.
  • 祭文 - A text used in rituals to honor the deceased.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 維某年月日 - Formal opening formula meaning 'On a certain day in a certain month of a certain year,' used to indicate the date of the document.
  • 驸马都尉 - Title given to the husband of an imperial princess, indicating his high status.
  • 关内河东副元帅 - Military title indicating the highest rank in the region of Guanxi and Hedong.
  • 司徒、兼中书令 - High-ranking official titles, indicating the holder's dual roles in both civil and military administration.
  • 汾阳郡王 - Noble title indicating the holder's status as a prince of Fenyang.
  • 敢昭告于 - Respectful formula meaning 'dare to inform respectfully to,' used to address a deity or a deceased person.
  • 德曜坤灵 - Phrase praising the virtue and beauty of the deceased, meaning 'virtue shines like the sky, beauty like the earth.'
  • 明齐月魄 - Phrase praising the clarity and brightness of the deceased, meaning 'brightness equals the moon.'
  • 母仪万国 - Phrase praising the deceased's role as a model mother, meaning 'model mother for all nations.'
  • 化洽六宫 - Phrase praising the deceased's influence, meaning 'influence reaches all palaces.'
  • 光辅圣人 - Phrase praising the deceased's support for the emperor, meaning 'brightly supporting the sage.'
  • 赞成阴教 - Phrase praising the deceased's role in women's education, meaning 'assisting in the education of women.'
  • 载荣史策 - Phrase meaning 'recorded in history,' indicating the deceased's importance.
  • 式播讴谣 - Phrase meaning 'spread in songs and poems,' indicating the widespread praise of the deceased.
  • 奄违圣日 - Phrase meaning 'suddenly departed from the world,' indicating the sudden death of the deceased.
  • 上仙灵界 - Phrase meaning 'ascended to the spiritual realm,' indicating the deceased's elevation to a divine status.
  • 遐迩痛愤 - Phrase meaning 'far and near grieve and anger,' indicating the widespread sorrow and anger.
  • 宫闱哀慕 - Phrase meaning 'the palace mourns and misses,' indicating the palace's deep sorrow.
  • 幸忝诸亲 - Phrase meaning 'lucky to be among the relatives,' indicating the writer's close relationship with the deceased.
  • 男尚贵主 - Phrase meaning 'my son is married to a princess,' indicating the writer's high social status.
  • 天人之美 - Phrase meaning 'beauty of heaven and earth,' indicating the deceased's exceptional qualities.
  • 鞠育所钟 - Phrase meaning 'nurtured and cherished,' indicating the deceased's care and upbringing.
  • 姻戚光荣 - Phrase meaning 'family and relatives are honored,' indicating the writer's family's prestige.
  • 宗族咸戴 - Phrase meaning 'all the clan members respect,' indicating the writer's clan's admiration.
  • 园陵礼备 - Phrase meaning 'the mausoleum ceremony is complete,' indicating the completion of the burial rites.
  • 祖载及期 - Phrase meaning 'the ancestral carriage arrives on time,' indicating the timely arrival of the funeral procession.
  • 限守方镇 - Phrase meaning 'limited by the duties of a regional governor,' indicating the writer's inability to attend the ceremony.
  • 不获陪侍行宫 - Phrase meaning 'unable to accompany and serve at the imperial palace,' indicating the writer's absence from the ceremony.
  • 瞻望灵驾 - Phrase meaning 'look forward to the spirit carriage,' indicating the writer's anticipation of the funeral procession.
  • 不胜摧慕 - Phrase meaning 'cannot bear the grief and longing,' indicating the writer's deep sorrow.
  • 伏荷皇恩 - Phrase meaning 'deeply grateful for the imperial favor,' indicating the writer's gratitude to the emperor.
  • 眷以国戚 - Phrase meaning 'graciously treated as a royal relative,' indicating the writer's high status.
  • 许申祭礼 - Phrase meaning 'allowed to perform the sacrificial rites,' indicating the writer's permission to conduct the funeral.
  • 超越等夷 - Phrase meaning 'exceeding the common people,' indicating the writer's special status.
  • 古今所绝 - Phrase meaning 'unprecedented in ancient and modern times,' indicating the uniqueness of the situation.
  • 独开圣造 - Phrase meaning 'only created by the sage,' indicating the writer's belief in the emperor's wisdom.
  • 无任惶恐铭戴之至 - Phrase meaning 'extremely fearful and grateful,' indicating the writer's deep respect and gratitude.
  • 谨献牲牢庶羞之奠 - Phrase meaning 'respectfully offer the sacrificial food and wine,' indicating the writer's offering to the deceased.
  • 尚飨 - Formal closing formula meaning 'may the spirit enjoy the offering,' used to conclude the document.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 驸马都尉 - Title given to the husband of an imperial princess, indicating his high status.
  • 关内河东副元帅 - Military title indicating the highest rank in the region of Guanxi and Hedong.
  • 司徒、兼中书令 - High-ranking official titles, indicating the holder's dual roles in both civil and military administration.
  • 汾阳郡王 - Noble title indicating the holder's status as a prince of Fenyang.
  • 贞懿皇后 - Title of the deceased, indicating her status as a queen with virtues of loyalty and integrity.
  • 园陵 - The mausoleum where the deceased is buried, indicating the grandeur of the burial.
  • 行宫 - The imperial palace, indicating the place where the funeral ceremony is held.
  • 牲牢庶羞之奠 - The sacrificial food and wine offered to the deceased, indicating the traditional Chinese funeral customs.

Proper Nouns

  • 郭暧 - The name of the writer's son, who is married to a princess.
  • 子仪 - The name of the writer, indicating his high status as a noble and official.
  • 傅涛 - The name of the official sent to inform the deceased.
  • 贞懿皇后 - The title of the deceased, indicating her status as a queen with virtues of loyalty and integrity.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兼连姻帝室 - Means 'also connected by marriage to the imperial family,' indicating a familial relationship between De Guo and the imperial family.
  • 门内休戚 - Means 'the joys and sorrows within the household.'
  • 爱姬 - Refers to beloved concubines or mistresses.
  • 竞宠争长 - Means 'competing for favor and status.'
  • 忿媢不相面 - Means 'resentful and not facing each other,' indicating a state of deep resentment and unwillingness to reconcile.
  • 尚父不能禁 - Means 'De Guo could not prevent it,' indicating his inability to control the situation.
  • 赐金帛及簪镮 - Means 'bestowed gold, silk, and jewelry,' referring to gifts given to resolve the conflict.
  • 命宫人载酒以和之 - Means 'ordered palace attendants to bring wine to reconcile them.'
  • 选人歌以送酒 - Means 'selected singers to perform songs to accompany the wine.'
  • 遽引蒲 - Means 'quickly pulled out a reed,' likely referring to a musical instrument.
  • 置觞于席前 - Means 'placed the cup before the seat,' indicating the act of pouring wine.
  • 酒尽不须歌 - Means 'no need for singing when the wine is finished,' indicating a refusal to engage in the reconciliation effort.
  • 郭暧尝与升平公主琴瑟不调 - Means 'De Guo Ai and Princess Shengping had a strained relationship,' referring to their marital discord.
  • 骂公主 - Means 'scolded the princess.'
  • 倚乃父为天子耶 - Means 'relying on your father being the emperor, is that it?'
  • 我父嫌天子不作 - Means 'my father regrets not being the emperor.'
  • 公主恚啼 - Means 'the princess was angry and wept.'
  • 奔车奏之 - Means 'rushed to the palace to report it.'
  • 谚云 - Means 'a saying goes.'
  • 不痴不聋，不作阿家阿翁 - A proverb meaning 'if one is not deaf or dumb, one cannot be a good father-in-law or mother-in-law,' implying that a father-in-law or mother-in-law should sometimes ignore trivial matters.
  • 小儿女子闺帏之言 - Means 'the words of children and women in the inner chambers.'
  • 大臣安用听 - Means 'why should ministers listen to such things?'
  • 锡赉以遣之 - Means 'bestowed gifts and sent him away.'
  • 尚父杖暧数十而已 - Means 'De Guo struck De Guo Ai dozens of times and then let him go.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 代宗 - Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty, known for his reign during a period of significant political and military challenges.
  • 郭尚父 - De Guo (郭子仪), a renowned general and statesman who played a crucial role in the Tang Dynasty's military and political affairs.
  • 升平公主 - Princess Shengping, a member of the Tang imperial family, known for her marriage to De Guo Ai.
  • 郭暧 - De Guo Ai, a son of De Guo, who had a strained relationship with his wife, Princess Shengping.
  • 中使内人 - Imperial messengers and palace attendants, who often carried messages and performed tasks on behalf of the emperor.
  • 爱姬 - Beloved concubines or mistresses, often influential figures in the lives of powerful men.
  • 忿媢不相面 - Indicates a state of deep resentment and unwillingness to reconcile, common in historical accounts of personal conflicts.
  • 赐金帛及簪镮 - Gifts of gold, silk, and jewelry, often used to resolve conflicts or show favor.
  • 命宫人载酒以和之 - A traditional method of reconciliation involving the sharing of wine and entertainment.
  • 选人歌以送酒 - Singers were often employed to entertain and soothe tensions.
  • 遽引蒲 - Refers to a musical instrument, likely a reed instrument, used in traditional Chinese music.
  • 置觞于席前 - Placing the wine cup before the seat, a gesture of respect and hospitality.
  • 酒尽不须歌 - A refusal to engage in the reconciliation effort, indicating a lack of willingness to participate.
  • 郭暧尝与升平公主琴瑟不调 - A common phrase used to describe a strained marital relationship.
  • 骂公主 - Scolding the princess, indicating a public display of anger.
  • 倚乃父为天子耶 - A rhetorical question implying that the princess's father is the emperor, suggesting a sense of privilege.
  • 我父嫌天子不作 - A statement expressing regret or dissatisfaction with not being the emperor.
  • 公主恚啼 - The princess's anger and weeping, indicating a strong emotional reaction.
  • 奔车奏之 - Rushing to the palace to report the incident, indicating a sense of urgency.
  • 谚云 - A proverb or saying, often used to convey wisdom or moral lessons.
  • 不痴不聋，不作阿家阿翁 - A proverb suggesting that a father-in-law or mother-in-law should sometimes ignore trivial matters to maintain harmony.
  • 小儿女子闺帏之言 - The words of children and women in the inner chambers, often considered trivial or insignificant.
  • 大臣安用听 - Why should ministers listen to such trivial matters, indicating a dismissive attitude.
  • 锡赉以遣之 - Bestowing gifts and sending someone away, often used to resolve conflicts or show leniency.
  • 尚父杖暧数十而已 - De Guo striking De Guo Ai dozens of times and then letting him go, indicating a form of punishment and reconciliation.

Proper Nouns

  • 代宗 - Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 郭尚父 - De Guo (郭子仪), a prominent general and statesman during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 升平公主 - Princess Shengping, a member of the Tang imperial family.
  • 郭暧 - De Guo Ai, a son of De Guo.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 深尚礼法 - Means 'highly valued ritual and law,' indicating a strong emphasis on proper conduct and legal adherence.
  • 召韩王食马齿羹，不设盐酪 - A specific act of humility and respect, where the new emperor invites the Prince of Han to eat a simple dish without seasoning or dairy products.
  • 皇姨有寡居者，时节入宫 - Describes the visit of the emperor's aunt to the palace during festivals.
  • 妆饰稍过，上见之极不悦 - Highlights the emperor's disapproval of excessive adornment.
  • 异日如礼，乃加敬焉 - Shows the emperor's respect for appropriate behavior.
  • 德宗初登勤政楼，外无知者 - Indicates that the people were unaware of the emperor's ascension to the throne.
  • 望见一人衣绿乘驴戴帽至楼下，仰视久之，俯而东去 - Describes a person dressed in green, riding a donkey and wearing a hat, who stops and looks up at the emperor's new residence before continuing eastward.
  • 上立遣宣示京尹，令以物色求之 - The emperor immediately ordered the capital's governor to search for the person based on his description.
  • 尹召万年捕贼官李镕，使促求访 - The governor called upon the official responsible for capturing criminals, Li Rong, to expedite the search.
  • 李尉伫立思之曰：“必得” - Li Rong stood thinking and confidently stated that he would find the person.
  • 及出，召干事、所由于春明门外数里内，应有诸司旧职事使艺人，悉搜罗之 - Upon leaving, he gathered all the officials and artists from various departments within several miles of Chunming Gate and searched them thoroughly.
  • 而绿衣者果在其中 - The person dressed in green was indeed among them.
  • 诘之，对曰：“某天宝教坊乐工也。上皇时，数登此。每来，鸱必集楼上，号随驾老鸱。某自罢居城外，更不复见。今群鸱盛集，又觉景象宛如昔时。心知圣人在上，悲喜且欲泣下。” - When questioned, the person replied that he was a musician from the Imperial Music Bureau during the Tianbao era, who often visited this place during the reign of the previous emperor. He explained that when he came, crows always gathered on the building, calling it the 'old crow following the carriage.' Since he moved to live outside the city, he no longer saw this. Now, seeing many crows gather again, he felt as if the past had returned, and he was moved to tears.
  • 以此奏闻 - This information was reported to the emperor.
  • 敕尽收此辈，却系教坊 - The emperor ordered that all such musicians be collected and returned to the Imperial Music Bureau.
  • 李尉亦为京尹所擢用，后至郡守 - Li Rong was promoted by the capital's governor and later became a prefect.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 德宗 - Refers to Emperor Dezong of Tang, who reigned from 779 to 805 AD.
  • 韩王 - The Prince of Han, a member of the imperial family.
  • 马齿羹 - A simple dish made from mallow leaves, symbolizing humility and simplicity.
  • 皇姨 - The emperor's aunt.
  • 勤政楼 - A building in the imperial palace where the emperor would conduct official business.
  • 京尹 - The governor of the capital city, responsible for maintaining order and security.
  • 万年捕贼官 - An official responsible for capturing criminals.
  • 春明门 - One of the gates of the capital city, Chang'an.
  • 教坊 - The Imperial Music Bureau, responsible for music and entertainment in the imperial court.
  • 天宝 - The era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, during which the Tang Dynasty reached its peak.
  • 上皇 - The previous emperor, referring to Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 鸱 - Crows, often associated with omens and significant events in Chinese culture.
  • 随驾老鸱 - A term used to describe the crows that gathered on the building during the reign of the previous emperor.

Proper Nouns

  • 德宗 - Emperor Dezong of Tang.
  • 韩王 - The Prince of Han.
  • 马齿羹 - A simple dish made from mallow leaves.
  • 皇姨 - The emperor's aunt.
  • 勤政楼 - A building in the imperial palace.
  • 京尹 - The governor of the capital city.
  • 万年捕贼官 - An official responsible for capturing criminals.
  • 春明门 - One of the gates of the capital city, Chang'an.
  • 教坊 - The Imperial Music Bureau.
  • 天宝 - The era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 上皇 - The previous emperor, referring to Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 鸱 - Crows.
  • 随驾老鸱 - A term used to describe the crows that gathered on the building during the reign of the previous emperor.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 與時候不相稱 - Means 'does not match the season'; indicates that the clothing is inappropriate for the current weather.
  • 編之於令 - Means 'to include it in the regulations'; suggests that the change would become official policy.
  • 上順天時，下盡物理 - Means 'follow the heavenly timing and fulfill the principles of nature'; justifies the emperor's decision.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 德宗 - Refers to Emperor Dezong of Tang (742-805), the 10th emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 翰林 - Refers to the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution of scholars and advisors in the imperial court.
  • 李趙公吉甫 - Refers to Li Jifu (758-814), a prominent statesman and scholar during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 李相程 - Refers to Premier Li Cheng, another high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 《月令》 - Refers to the *Lüli* (Rites of Zhou), a classical text that outlines the proper rituals and practices for each month of the year.
  • 玄宗皇帝 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang (685-762), the 8th emperor of the Tang Dynasty, known for his cultural and administrative reforms.

Proper Nouns

  • 德宗 - Emperor Dezong of Tang.
  • 李趙公吉甫 - Li Jifu.
  • 李相程 - Premier Li Cheng.
  • 《月令》 - *Lüli* (Rites of Zhou).
  • 玄宗皇帝 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 索前史及诸家并佛经，多作口边敢 - Searching through historical records, various texts, and Buddhist scriptures, it is often written as '口边敢.'
  • 其音与著两火同徒敢反 - Its pronunciation is the same as '著两火' (two fires), which is pronounced '徒敢反' (tu gan fan).
  • 又《玉篇》、《切韵》，啖字是正也，著两火俗也，并徒敢反，正合作啖不疑矣 - According to *Yupian* and *Qieyun* (ancient dictionaries), '啖' is the correct form, while '著两火' is a common variant, both pronounced '徒敢反.' It should be written as '啖' without doubt.
  • 啗马即合用啗字，音徒滥反 - For '啗马' (feeding a horse), the character '啗' should be used, pronounced '徒滥反' (tu lan fan).
  • 以义言之，以物自食，谓之啖 - By definition, eating something by oneself is called '啖.'
  • 以物喂人，谓之啗 - Feeding someone else is also called '啗.'
  • 又案《汉书 高帝纪》云：“使郦食其、陆贾往说，将啗以利。” - According to the *Book of Han: Annals of Emperor Gao*, it says, 'Send Li Siqi and Lu Jia to persuade, and offer them benefits to entice them.'
  • 颜监注曰：“啗者本谓食啗耳，音徒敢反，以食喂人，令其啗食，则改变为徒滥反。今以利诱之，取实为辞也。” - The commentary by Yan Shigu says, '啗 originally means to eat, pronounced '徒敢反.' When used to feed someone, it changes to '徒滥反.' Now, it is used to entice with benefits, taking the actual meaning as the basis.'
  • 颜监讲啗字虽未分明，亦无口傍两火之说 - Although Yan Shigu's explanation is not clear, he does not mention the character '口傍两火' (two fires on the side of the mouth).
  • 则明是字俗相传，而后人为姓，亦因俗耳 - This clearly shows that the character has been passed down through common usage, and later it became a surname due to this tradition.
  • 近日书饼啖字，至有食边口边作覃，及口边作詹者，率意而为，其误甚矣 - Recently, when writing '饼啖' (cakes and eating), some people have written '食边口边作覃' (food side and mouth side as Tan) and '口边作詹' (mouth side as Zhan), which are arbitrary and very incorrect.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long descriptive sentence '漢州開元寺，有菩薩像。自頂及焰光坐趺，都是一段青石。潔腻可愛，雕琢極工，高數尺，會昌毀寺時，佛像多遭摧折刓缺，惟此不傷' must be read as a single unit, detailing the statue's appearance and its survival during the temple's destruction.
  • The clause '及再立寺，僧振古寶而置放西廊' describes the monks' actions after the temple's rebuilding, linking the statue's preservation to its placement.
  • The complex sentence '余與京大德知玄法事西川従事揚仁赡同谒' introduces the narrator and other visitors, requiring careful parsing to maintain clarity.
  • The clause '楊深于釋氏，好古之士也' provides background on Yang's knowledge and interests, linking his character to the context of the visit.
  • The clause '赡敬弥日，而玄心精识多闻，话其本末云' describes Yang's behavior and the monk Xuan's knowledge, setting the stage for the story that follows.
  • The clause '先是匠人得此石异之，虔心镌刻，殆忘餐寝' introduces the craftsman's dedication and the supernatural element of the story.
  • The clause '有美女常器食给之，其人运思在像，都无邪思' describes the craftsman's focus and the mysterious woman's role.
  • The clause '久之，怠而妄心生，女乃不至，饥渴既逼，兼毒厉匝体，遂悟是天女' narrates the craftsman's downfall and his realization of the woman's divine nature.
  • The clause '因焚香叩首，悔谢切至，女复来，其病立愈，而像即成' describes the craftsman's repentance and the statue's completion.
  • The clause '亦尝有记录，因毁寺失其传焉，寺今再立矣' provides historical context and concludes the narrative with the temple's rebuilding.
